title: STAT+: Trump administration prepares 100% tariffs on some imported drugs
summary: The White House is readying a 100% tax on some imported medicines, a draft order shows.

title: STAT+: Makary marks one year at FDA with focus on achievements in speech to staff
summary: The FDA commissioner praised staff and listed his accomplishments in a speech to workers.

title: Biogen, on the heels of a $5.6B buyout, turns its sights to early-stage assets
summary: In an interview, Chief Financial Officer Robin Kramer said the combined cash flow from Biogen and Apellis will not only help debt leverage ratios but also free up capacity to do other strategic deals.

title: STAT+: Large AI scribe study finds modest time savings, inconsistent use
summary: AI scribes saved doctors, across five academic medical centers, about 16 minutes per eight hours of patient care, a new study found.

title: STAT+: Government watchdog urges FDA to finalize guidance for advisory committee conflicts of interest
summary: The FDA has often failed to share information on how it determines whether advisory committee members have financial conflicts of interest, according to a new review.

title: FDA approves Lilly obesity pill, triggering battle with Novo Nordisk
summary: Foundayo could erase the Wegovy pill’s four-month head start as the two drugmakers clash on a debate of efficacy and convenience.

title: Eli Lilly’s obesity pill approved by FDA, setting up fierce competition with Novo Nordisk
summary: Eli Lilly’s obesity pill was approved by the FDA, setting it up for fierce competition against Novo Nordisk’s new Wegovy pill.

title: FDA Approves First New Molecular Entity Under National Priority Voucher Program
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Foundayo (orforglipron) marking the fifth approval under the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher (CNPV) pilot program.

title: STAT+: Insilico Medicine CEO on how best to use AI in drug development
summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: How to be a 'productive' biotech AI company, with Insilico Medicine CEO Alex Zhavoronkov, and more.

title: Ambrosia adds a megaround for obesity drugs
summary: The $100 million Series B round will be used to bring the biotech’s oral GLP-1 into early human testing.

title: STAT+: Novo’s subscription model raises questions about pharma-telehealth tie-ups
summary: A delayed FDA decision for Orca Bio, new questions about pharma-telehealth tie-ups, and more

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA view on peptide compounding, Iran war hurting trial recruitment, and more
summary: The FDA is moving toward allowing compounding pharmacies to produce injectable peptides that were banned over significant safety concerns

title: A ‘hijacked plane’: CDC, under RFK Jr.’s influence, trades science for dogma
summary: A series of controversial policy changes directed by the HHS secretary have left experts fearful of the future implications for public health.

title: FDA, after turbulent year, leaves drugmakers guessing on its direction
summary: Constant leadership changes and erratic decision-making have left the biopharmaceutical industry feeling its chief regulator is as unpredictable as it’s ever been.

title: Supreme Court rules against conversion therapy ban
summary: Fact-checking Jay Bhattacharya, why PCOS might get a new name, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Can a psychedelic ‘coach’ make ketamine therapy even more effective?
summary: Should ketamine be used strictly as a pharmacological intervention, or should psychotherapy be incorporated? A new study is trying to tackle this question.

title: STAT+: NIH director’s invocation of Vannevar Bush’s legacy to promote Trump science policies sparks pushback
summary: Jay Bhattacharya’s claim that the Trump administration is pursuing the vision articulated by scientific leader, Vannevar Bush, has provoked pushback — and outrage

title: STAT+: The biotech scorecard for the second quarter: 23 stock-moving events to watch
summary: Here is STAT’s biotech scorecard, our regular ledger of stock-moving biotech events, for the second quarter.

title: Why an ovary syndrome may get a new name: Men seem to have PCOS, too
summary: A quiet effort to change the name of PCOS is nearing completion, driven in part by growing evidence of a male version.

title: Opinion: Medical marijuana should not be recommended for PTSD, anxiety, or depression
summary: “Rigorously derived evidence for the medical efficacy of marijuana is thin,” writes Kevin Sabet.

title: Opinion: Medicare is restricting care for 1 million Americans based on a faulty assumption. Congress must intervene
summary: Medicare is restricting care for 1 million Americans based on a faulty assumption.

title: STAT+: The HSA industry makes its MAHA pitch
summary: A new lobbying group tied to the health savings account industry is pushing hard to make HSAs far more widely available.

title: Lilly beefs up neuroscience pipeline with $6.3B Centessa buyout
summary: The deal secures two sleep disorder drugs that would fill a hole in the company’s portfolio and intensify a competition with Takeda and Eisai.

title: STAT+: HHS changes the U.S. health IT office’s name, and its purview
summary: The Trump administration is changing the name of the federal health IT office back to the Office of the National Coordinator.

title: STAT+: Baby KJ scientists hit speed bump in quest to scale custom gene editor
summary: The scientists who treated Baby KJ say the FDA's strict standards will make it much harder to create bespoke gene editing for others.

title: Viridian tumbles on positive data for key eye drug prospect
summary: The data did not meet investor expectations, and could bring “fierce investor debate on commercial feasibility,” according to an analyst.

title: Supreme Court rules against Colorado ban on ‘conversion therapy’ for LGBTQ kids
summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday ruled against a law banning “conversion therapy” for LGBTQ+ kids in Colorado, one of about two dozen states that ban the discredited practice.

title: STAT+: OpenEvidence inks deal with major health system
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: New OpenEvidence's partnership, pressure on HHS over health record sharing, and K health has a bot.

title: Biogen, with $5.6B Apellis buy, builds out immunology offerings
summary: Chief Financial Officer Robin Kramer explained how the deal not only provides two marketed products, but a team with nephrology expertise that will be valuable as Biogen prepares to launch…

title: Merck strikes deal with antibody discovery startup
summary: In an R&D collaboration worth as much as $838 million, Merck will lean on Infinimmune to discover immune cell-directed antibodies for multiple targets.

title: mRNA is poised to rise beyond infectious diseases, if it’s not derailed by R&D cuts
summary: Research into areas like cancer could become collateral damage of a broader anti-mRNA push, according to a new study.

FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Kresladi (marnetegragene autotemcel), the first gene therapy for the treatment of severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I (LAD-I).
